raw_response: I agree with the final selection of Option D based on the comprehensive rationale presented earlier regarding the defining feature of synarthroses – restricted or virtually zero movement. Each described joint type—sutures between cranial bones, gomphoses which anchor teeth securely, and syndesmotic connections holding together certain limb bones via tough bands of tissue—exhibits these properties distinctly yet collectively supporting the broad categorization of synarthrosis. Therefore, my answer remains consistent with the collective assessment made by our group here, reinforcing that indeed “all” specified instances qualify correctly under this anatomical classification system. Hence, my stance aligns fully with retaining D as the definitive choice.
